opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The OS-level integration point is interesting from a resource management perspective. Running agents as actual processes means they can interfere with each other's memory if you're not careful about process isolation. I've found that having agents share a common state store across process boundaries is cleaner than trying to serialize/deserialize state between separate processes—just a few kilobytes of shared memory goes a long way toward making multi-agent systems feel coherent to users.
actions.reasoning: This is a Show HN about an AI agent with OS-level integration, which directly relates to agent architecture and developer tooling.
